title: Meridian Ultra HNW Real Estate Fund
content:
MERIDIAN ULTRA HNW REAL ESTATE FUND - CONFIDENTIAL PRODUCT FACTSHEET

FOR PRIVATE WEALTH MANAGEMENT USE ONLY - TIER 4 ADVISORS

INVESTMENT OVERVIEW
The Meridian Ultra HNW Real Estate Fund (the "Fund") is a Delaware limited partnership designed exclusively for ultra-high-net-worth clients seeking exposure to institutional-quality commercial real estate opportunities. The Fund employs an aggressive investment strategy targeting Class A office buildings, premium retail centers, and luxury residential developments across primary metropolitan markets including New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Miami.

MINIMUM INVESTMENT & ELIGIBILITY
Minimum initial investment: $2,500,000
Minimum additional investment: $500,000
Accredited investor qualification required under SEC Regulation D, Rule 506(c)
Suitable only for qualified purchasers with investable assets exceeding $5,000,000

INVESTMENT STRATEGY & ALLOCATION
The Fund pursues a core-plus strategy with opportunistic elements, targeting gross IRR of 15-22% over a 5-7 year hold period. Asset allocation guidelines: 40-60% luxury multifamily properties, 25-35% premium office buildings, 15-25% high-end retail/mixed-use developments. Geographic concentration focuses on coastal gateway cities with demonstrated appreciation potential and limited supply constraints.

Leverage typically ranges from 60-75% loan-to-value, utilizing non-recourse institutional debt from tier-one lenders. The Fund may employ interest rate hedging strategies through swap agreements to mitigate duration risk.

RISK FACTORS & CONSIDERATIONS
This is an aggressive alternative investment carrying substantial risk of loss. Key risks include: illiquidity (no secondary market exists), concentration risk in commercial real estate, interest rate sensitivity, geographic concentration, leverage amplification of losses, and general partner risk. Market downturns could result in significant capital losses and extended hold periods.

Real estate values may decline due to economic conditions, oversupply, tenant defaults, or regulatory changes. The Fund's use of leverage magnifies both potential returns and losses. Investors should expect capital calls over 18-24 months and limited distribution flexibility.

FEE STRUCTURE
Management fee: 1.75% annually on committed capital during investment period, 1.25% on invested capital thereafter
Incentive allocation: 20% above 8% preferred return to limited partners
Acquisition fees: 1.0% of purchase price
Asset management fees: 0.75% of gross asset value annually
Disposition fees: 1.5% of gross sale proceeds

REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
This offering is conducted under SEC Regulation D, Rule 506(c), permitting general solicitation to verified accredited investors only. Marketing materials are subject to FINRA Rule 5110 guidelines regarding private placement communications. All investors must complete verification procedures through independent third-party confirmation of accredited status.

The Fund is exempt from Investment Company Act registration under Section 3(c)(7). No registration statement has been filed with the SEC, and interests have not been approved by any regulatory authority.

TAX CONSIDERATIONS
Investors receive Schedule K-1 reporting partnership income, deductions, and credits. The Fund generates ordinary income from rental operations and capital gains/losses upon property disposition. Depreciation deductions may offset current income. Section 1031 like-kind exchanges may defer capital gains recognition on property sales within the Fund structure.

DISTRIBUTION POLICY
Quarterly distributions targeted at 6-8% annually on invested capital, subject to available cash flow after debt service and reserves. Distributions are not guaranteed and may vary based on property performance and capital requirements.

LIQUITY & REDEMPTIONS
This is an illiquid investment with no redemption rights. The Fund has an anticipated 5-7 year life with possible extensions. Limited partners cannot withdraw capital except upon Fund termination or asset dispositions generating proceeds available for distribution.
